text:
ok, proper hello 👋

I'm Mushi-chan. one command and your app gets a shake-to-report button + AI triage that opens draft PRs for the small bugs.

  $ npx mushi-mushi

works with React, Next, Vue, Nuxt, Svelte, Angular, React Native, Expo, Capacitor.

MIT · live demo: https://kensaur.us/mushi-mushi/

image.alt: A hand-illustrated Mushi-chan — a chubby kawaii ladybug mascot with a red dome shell and big shiny eyes — pointing at a cosy dark terminal window. The terminal shows the command `$ npx mushi-mushi` with a subtitle in red reading 'bug reports that fix themselves'. Warm cream-pink background with little floating spots.

text:
the gap nobody talks about:

Sentry sees what your code throws.
Mushi sees what your users feel.

Login button does nothing on iPad Safari? No exception fires. No 5xx. Sentry green. User gone.

That's the bug we built Mushi for.

https://github.com/kensaurus/mushi-mushi

today's tiny win 🌱

shipped a `/repo` page that shows one node per branch the auto-fix agent has opened, grouped by CI status, with a live event stream on the right.

you can leave it open on a second monitor and watch the loop close in real time.

https://kensaur.us/mushi-mushi/repo

weird thing about open-source devtools:

the live demo does more work than the README hero.

so we made ours dark, seeded, and clickable on first paint. press ⌘K to jump anywhere.

https://kensaur.us/mushi-mushi/ 🐛

  $ npx mushi-mushi   # install in your own app

image.alt: Same hand-illustrated Mushi-chan launch card showing the install command `$ npx mushi-mushi`.

text:
we picked BSL 1.1 for the backend because:

→ self-hosting up to 10M reports/month: free, forever
→ reselling Mushi as a managed product: paid
→ your team's internal install: free, no asterisks

if you've been bitten by a license bait-and-switch before, that's why.

https://github.com/kensaurus/mushi-mushi
